serviceDescription: Amazon OpenSearch Service is a fully managed service that makes it easy for you to deploy, secure, and operate OpenSearch clusters at scale.

components.label: Amazon OpenSearch Service data instance cost

row.label: Utilization (On-Demand only)
row.description: Data Instances will be always at 100% utilization dedicated for your Amazon Opensearch Service domain, to stop incurring charges you must Delete the domain.

Amazon OpenSearch Service dedicated master instance cost

row.label: Utilization (On-Demand only)
row.description: Dedicated Master Instances will be always at 100% utilization dedicated for your Amazon Opensearch Service domain, to stop incurring charges you can either reduce or remove the number of Dedicated Master Instances from the domain or Delete the domain.

components.label: Amazon OpenSearch Service dedicated master instance cost
Amazon OpenSearch Service dedicated master instance cost

cards.title: Storage
cards.description: Enter the amount of storage you'd like for each data instance. If you choose the EBS storage type, the calculation below multiplies EBS storage size per instance by the number of data instances in your cluster to calculate the total storage cost for the cluster. Storage settings do not apply to any dedicated master instances in the cluster.

components.label: Number of instances
components.description: Enter the total number of data instances using EBS

components.label: Storage for each Amazon OpenSearch Service instance
info alert
components.content: OR1 instances are provisioned with EBS and managed storage. Data written to EBS is also synchronously written to the managed storage. So for example if you write 100 GB to the EBS volume you will have 100 GB also written to the Managed Storage. If you configure 1 Primary and 1 replica then total local storage will be 200 GB and 100 GB in managed storage.

components.label: info alert
components.content: Volume type selected supports storage amounts 10 GB - 24,000 GB per volume, IOPS 3,000 - 16,000 per volume, and throughput 125 MB/s - 1000 MB/s per volume

components.label: Storage amount per volume (gp3)
components.description: gp3 supports a minimum of 10 GB and a maximum of 24000 GB per volume.

components.label: Provisioning IOPS per volume (gp3)
components.description: gp3 supports a maximum of 16,000 IOPS per volume.

components.header: Provision IOPs per volume should exceed the minimum IOPs which is up to 16,000 IOPS for free:
components.label: info alert
components.content:
For gp3 storage amount per volume entered between 10 GB and 1000 GB , the minimum is 3000 IOPS.

For gp3 storage amount per volume entered greater than 1000 GB, the minimum IOPS is equal to 3 * gp3 storage amount per volume and up to 16,000 IOPS.

components.label: info alert
components.content: The maximum ratio of provisioned IOPS to provisioned volume size is 500 IOPS per GB.

components.label: Provisioning throughput (MB/s) per volume (gp3)
components.description: gp3 supports a maximum of 1000 MBps per volume.

components.header: Provision throughput per volume should exceed the minimum throughput which is up to 1000 MB/s:
components.label: info alert
components.content:
For gp3 storage amount per volume entered less than or equal to 170 GB , the minimum throughput for free is 125 MB/s.

For gp3 storage amount per volume entered greater than 170 GB, every 3000 GB volumes will get incremental 250 MB/s as minimum for free and it is up to 1000 MB/s.

components.header: Invalid IOPS to Provisioned Throughput ratio
components.label: info alert
components.content: The provisioned Throughput to provisioned IOPS ratio should not exceed 0.25 MBs/IOPS.
